frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: decoding distributed fMRI patterns to read out working-memory contents across brain regions

frameworks.name: inverted encoding model
frameworks.one_line: computational reconstruction of feature-specific channel responses from voxel patterns

frameworks.name: visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: dissociating neural substrates for attended, prospectively-relevant, and discarded items

core_question: What neural mechanisms distinguish currently-attended, future-relevant, and discarded items in visual working memory?
key_assumptions: feature-specific WM contents are decodable from BOLD patterns
inverted encoding models recover channel-tuned representations
attended, prospective, and discarded items occupy distinct functional states
manipulations: relevance status of held items (current vs future vs discarded)
instructed forgetting vs prospective retention cues
key_findings: currently-relevant items decodable in visual, parietal, and posterior frontal cortex
deliberate forgetting suppresses discarded representations in early visual cortex
future-relevant information is neither actively maintained nor inhibited in those regions
interpretations: distinct neural mechanisms support attended versus prospectively-held WM contents
discarding is implemented via active suppression in early visual cortex
